title: feed.rs: redundant sanitize_html pass on already-safe link HTML
description:
LOCATION: src/feed.rs:65-67 and 75-77 FINDING: link_html is built by format!() using escape_html_attr(url) as the only variable content. The URL is then immediately passed through sanitize_html(&link_html). The sanitize_html call invokes ammonia, which runs a full html5ever parse-and-serialize cycle. This is redundant: escape_html_attr already neutralises all injection vectors in the URL, and the surrounding

structure is a literal string with no variables. Same pattern repeated at line 77 for the 'read more' link after truncation. IMPACT: Two unnecessary html5ever parse+serialize round-trips per feed entry with a link — ammonia is not a trivial call (it runs Servo's html5ever tokenizer under the hood). The result is identical to the input in both cases.
